title: ilex pedunculosa f. aurantiaca
intro: Ilex pedunculosa f. aurantiaca, also known as the Yellow-berried Holly, is a variety of the Ilex pedunculosa plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the family Aquifoliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Australia and other parts of the world, for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The Yellow-berried Holly is said to have several health benefits, including its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and protect against certain types of cancer. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
